title: June in Tauranga
meta_desc: Discover Tauranga and Mount Maunganui in June with kids. Weather, events, and tips for a fun family holiday in NZ's most popular beach town.
intro: Tauranga and Mount Maunganui are a classic Kiwi summer holiday spot, but June is a great time to visit with kids. The weather is mild, and the crowds are smaller. You can enjoy the beautiful beaches, scenic walks, and family-friendly activities without the peak season prices. In this guide, we'll cover what to expect in June, what's on, and some practical tips for a fun and stress-free family holiday.

sections.heading: Weather in June
sections.body:
June is the start of winter in New Zealand, but Tauranga and Mount Maunganui have a mild climate. Average highs are around 17°C, while average lows are around 9°C. It's a great time to enjoy the outdoors, with fewer crowds and lower prices.

Be prepared for some rain, with an average of 12 rainy days in June. But don't let that stop you - there are plenty of indoor activities to enjoy, like the Hot Pools at the Mount or a visit to the Tauranga Art Gallery.

sections.heading: What's On in June
sections.body:
June is a relatively quiet month in Tauranga and Mount Maunganui, but there are still some great events to check out. The Tauranga Winter Festival is a highlight, with live music, food stalls, and activities for kids.

If you're visiting in late June, you might catch the start of the school holidays, which means more families and kids around. But don't worry, there's still plenty of room on the beach and in the town.
list: School holidays start in late June

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
June is a great time to enjoy the outdoors in Tauranga and Mount Maunganui. The weather is mild, and the crowds are smaller, making it perfect for activities like surfing, walking, and beach time.

Some of the best activities for June include:
list: Surfing lessons at Mount Beach ($60-$80 per person)
Walking to the summit of Mount Maunganui (free)
Visiting the Hot Pools at the Mount ($20-$30 per person)

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
June is a relatively quiet month in Tauranga and Mount Maunganui, but it's still a good idea to book ahead for popular activities and accommodations. The town can get busy on weekends and during school holidays, so plan ahead to avoid disappointment.

Book your surf lessons, Hot Pools visit, and accommodations at least a week in advance to ensure availability.
list: Book surf lessons at least a week in advance
Book Hot Pools visit at least a week in advance
Book accommodations at least a week in advance

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body: Here are some practical tips for families visiting Tauranga and Mount Maunganui in June:
list: Pack warm clothing for cooler mornings and evenings
Bring a rain jacket or umbrella
Take advantage of free activities like walking to the summit of Mount Maunganui
Book a family-friendly accommodation with a kitchen to save money on food
Check the surf forecast before heading to the beach
